name: Chicken Fingers from Joan Johnson
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the chicken breasts of any excess fat and cut each into 8 even strips.
2. In a bowl, combine the chicken strips and yogurt. Stir gently to coat each piece evenly.
3. In a shallow dish or plate, mix together the crushed soda crackers, thyme, marjoram, curry powder, and salt.
4. Place each chicken strip in the seasoned crumbs and roll firmly to coat.
5. Arrange the coated chicken strips on a cake rack set in a baking dish or pan, forming a single layer.
6. Bake at 375°F for 25 minutes, or until the crumbs are lightly browned and crisp. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with salt to taste.
Dipping Sauce:
1/2 cup Low fat yogurt
2 Tbsp Catsup
2 Tbsp Finely chopped celery
1 tsp Light soy sauce
1/4 tsp Very finely minced garlic
Freshly ground pepper, to taste.
Makes 3 servings, 8 fingers each.
